joined_reference: it confirms fincher 's status as a film maker who artfully bends technical know-how to the service of psychological insight .  too much of it feels unfocused and underdeveloped .  a great ensemble cast ca n't lift this heartfelt enterprise out of the familiar .  prurient playthings aside , there 's little to love about this english trifle .  it moves quickly , adroitly , and without fuss ; it does n't give you time to reflect on the inanity -- and the cold war datedness -- of its premise .  it proves quite compelling as an intense , brooding character study .  bogdanovich tantalizes by offering a peep show into the lives of the era 's creme de la celluloid .  looks and feels like a project better suited for the small screen .  verbinski implements every hack-artist trick to give us the ooky-spookies .  this is a good script , good dialogue , funny even for adults .  a painfully funny ode to bad behavior .  there is a fabric of complex ideas here , and feelings that profoundly deepen them .  the overall effect is less like a children 's movie than a recruitment film for future hollywood sellouts .  as the two leads , lathan and diggs are charming and have chemistry both as friends and lovers .  the title not only describes its main characters , but the lazy people behind the camera as well .  zhang ... has done an amazing job of getting realistic performances from his mainly nonprofessional cast .  not only is undercover brother as funny , if not more so , than both austin powers films , but it 's also one of the smarter , savvier spoofs to come along in some time .  beautifully observed , miraculously unsentimental comedy-drama .  the last 20 minutes are somewhat redeeming , but most of the movie is the same teenage american road-trip drek we 've seen before - only this time you have to read the fart jokes  if you dig on david mamet 's mind tricks ... rent this movie and enjoy !  for all its impressive craftsmanship , and despite an overbearing series of third-act crescendos , lily chou-chou never really builds up a head of emotional steam .  yes , dull .  shaky close-ups of turkey-on-rolls , stubbly chins , liver spots , red noses and the filmmakers new bobbed do draw easy chuckles but lead nowhere .  the second coming of harry potter is a film far superior to its predecessor .  if you 're hard up for raunchy college humor , this is your ticket right here .  nonsensical , dull `` cyber-horror '' flick is a grim , hollow exercise in flat scares and bad acting .  a delectable and intriguing thriller filled with surprises , read my lips is an original .  thanks to scott 's charismatic roger and eisenberg 's sweet nephew , roger dodger is one of the most compelling variations on in the company of men .  there 's no emotional pulse to solaris .  a fast , funny , highly enjoyable movie .  too restrained to be a freak show , too mercenary and obvious to be cerebral , too dull and pretentious to be engaging ... the isle defies an easy categorization .  an unwise amalgam of broadcast news and vibes .  a pleasant enough romance with intellectual underpinnings , the kind of movie that entertains even as it turns maddeningly predictable .  it 's not original , and , robbed of the element of surprise , it does n't have any huge laughs in its story of irresponsible cops who love to play pranks .  it 's hard to imagine alan arkin being better than he is in this performance .  dazzles with its fully-written characters , its determined stylishness ( which always relates to characters and story ) and johnny dankworth 's best soundtrack in years .  fun , flip and terribly hip bit of cinematic entertainment .  the movie fails to live up to the sum of its parts .  one of the more intelligent children 's movies to hit theaters this year .  corpus collosum -- while undeniably interesting -- wore out its welcome well before the end credits rolled about 45 minutes in .  the performances take the movie to a higher level .  one of those pictures whose promising , if rather precious , premise is undercut by amateurish execution .  a compelling spanish film about the withering effects of jealousy in the life of a young monarch whose sexual passion for her husband becomes an obsession .  uses high comedy to evoke surprising poignance .  it has all the excitement of eating oatmeal .  feature debuter d.j. caruso directs a crack ensemble cast , bringing screenwriter tony gayton 's narcotics noir to life .  the old-world - meets-new mesh is incarnated in the movie 's soundtrack , a joyful effusion of disco bollywood that , by the end of monsoon wedding , sent my spirit soaring out of the theater .  an important movie , a reminder of the power of film to move us and to make us examine our values .  it 's a bit disappointing that it only manages to be decent instead of dead brilliant .  it 's not the ultimate depression-era gangster movie .  it has the charm of the original american road movies , feasting on the gorgeous , ramshackle landscape of the filmmaker 's motherland .  the vitality of the actors keeps the intensity of the film high , even as the strafings blend together .  the movie is n't just hilarious : it 's witty and inventive , too , and in hindsight , it is n't even all that dumb .  it 's difficult to imagine the process that produced such a script , but here 's guessing that spray cheese and underarm noises played a crucial role .  while locals will get a kick out of spotting cleveland sites , the rest of the world will enjoy a fast-paced comedy with quirks that might make the award-winning coen brothers envious .  broomfield turns his distinctive ` blundering ' style into something that could really help clear up the case .  oh come on .  awesome creatures , breathtaking scenery , and epic battle scenes add up to another ` spectacular spectacle . '  paid in full is so stale , in fact , that its most vibrant scene is one that uses clips from brian de palma 's scarface .  warm water under a red bridge is a quirky and poignant japanese film that explores the fascinating connections between women , water , nature , and sexuality .  the wild thornberrys movie is a jolly surprise .  the film flat lines when it should peak and is more missed opportunity and trifle than dark , decadent truffle .  for the first time in years , de niro digs deep emotionally , perhaps because he 's been stirred by the powerful work of his co-stars .  escaping the studio , piccoli is warmly affecting and so is this adroitly minimalist movie .

joined_reference: a moody , multi-dimensional love story and sci-fi mystery , solaris is a thought-provoking , haunting film that allows the seeds of the imagination to germinate .  a celebration of quirkiness , eccentricity , and certain individuals ' tendency to let it all hang out , and damn the consequences .  harris commands the screen , using his frailty to suggest the ravages of a life of corruption and ruthlessness .  it does nothing new with the old story , except to show fisticuffs in this sort of stop-go slow motion that makes the gang rumbles look like they 're being streamed over a 28k modem .  martin and barbara are complex characters -- sometimes tender , sometimes angry -- and the delicate performances by sven wollter and viveka seldahl make their hopes and frustrations vivid .  as a first-time director , paxton has tapped something in himself as an actor that provides frailty with its dark soul .  the result is a gaudy bag of stale candy , something from a halloween that died .  it is great summer fun to watch arnold and his buddy gerald bounce off a quirky cast of characters .  the best revenge may just be living well because this film , unlike other dumas adaptations , is far more likened to a treasure than a lengthy jail sentence .  sticky sweet sentimentality , clumsy plotting and a rosily myopic view of life in the wwii-era mississippi delta undermine this adaptation .  even if you do n't think ( kissinger 's ) any more guilty of criminal activity than most contemporary statesmen , he 'd sure make a courtroom trial great fun to watch .  forced , familiar and thoroughly condescending .  this is a winning ensemble comedy that shows canadians can put gentle laughs and equally gentle sentiments on the button , just as easily as their counterparts anywhere else in the world .  scores no points for originality , wit , or intelligence .  an entertaining , colorful , action-filled crime story with an intimate heart .  manages to be sweet and wickedly satisfying at the same time .  i got a headache watching this meaningless downer .  it 's a stunning lyrical work of considerable force and truth .  i 've always dreamed of attending cannes , but after seeing this film , it 's not that big a deal .  so much facile technique , such cute ideas , so little movie .  the movie is dawn of the dead crossed with john carpenter 's ghosts of mars , with zombies not as ghoulish as the first and trains not as big as the second .  just not campy enough  when leguizamo finally plugged an irritating character late in the movie .  visually imaginative , thematically instructive and thoroughly delightful , it takes us on a roller-coaster ride from innocence to experience without even a hint of that typical kiddie-flick sentimentality .  ( e ) ventually , every idea in this film is flushed down the latrine of heroism .  the story and structure are well-honed .  for close to two hours the audience is forced to endure three terminally depressed , mostly inarticulate , hyper dysfunctional families for the price of one .  a rarity among recent iranian films : it 's a comedy full of gentle humor that chides the absurdity of its protagonist 's plight .  / but daphne , you 're too buff / fred thinks he 's tough / and velma - wow , you 've lost weight !  `` mostly martha '' is a bright , light modern day family parable that wears its heart on its sleeve for all to see .  a bloated gasbag thesis grotesquely impressed by its own gargantuan aura of self-importance ...  too slow , too long and too little happens .  not only are the special effects and narrative flow much improved , and daniel radcliffe more emotionally assertive this time around as harry , but the film conjures the magic of author j.k. rowling 's books .  has all the depth of a wading pool .  even in its most tedious scenes , russian ark is mesmerizing .  on this tricky topic , tadpole is very much a step in the right direction , with its blend of frankness , civility and compassion .  candid and comfortable ; a film that deftly balances action and reflection as it lets you grasp and feel the passion others have for their work .  this is human comedy at its most amusing , interesting and confirming .  in an effort , i suspect , not to offend by appearing either too serious or too lighthearted , it offends by just being wishy-washy .  you wonder why enough was n't just a music video rather than a full-length movie .  this is not the undisputed worst boxing movie ever , but it 's certainly not a champion - the big loser is the audience .  ... a fun little timewaster , helped especially by the cool presence of jean reno .  mcconaughey 's fun to watch , the dragons are okay , not much fire in the script .  there is nothing outstanding about this film , but it is good enough and will likely be appreciated most by sailors and folks who know their way around a submarine .  care deftly captures the wonder and menace of growing up , but he never really embraces the joy of fuhrman 's destructive escapism or the grace-in-rebellion found by his characters .  less dizzying than just dizzy , the jaunt is practically over before it begins .  a coarse and stupid gross-out .  serving sara does n't serve up a whole lot of laughs .  writer/director joe carnahan 's grimy crime drama is a manual of precinct cliches , but it moves fast enough to cover its clunky dialogue and lapses in logic .  this is an egotistical endeavor from the daughter of horror director dario argento ( a producer here ) , but her raw performance and utter fearlessness make it strangely magnetic .  the movie does a good job of laying out some of the major issues that we encounter as we journey through life .  a hamfisted romantic comedy that makes our girl the hapless facilitator of an extended cheap shot across the mason-dixon line .  sustains its dreamlike glide through a succession of cheesy coincidences and voluptuous cheap effects , not the least of which is rebecca romijn-stamos .  it inspires a continuing and deeply satisfying awareness of the best movies as monumental ` picture shows . '  nothing is sacred in this gut-buster .  it 's hampered by a lifetime-channel kind of plot and a lead actress who is out of her depth .  and the lesson , in the end , is nothing new .  aside from minor tinkering , this is the same movie you probably loved in 1994 , except that it looks even better .  brilliantly explores the conflict between following one 's heart and following the demands of tradition .  a rigorously structured and exquisitely filmed drama about a father and son connection that is a brief shooting star of love .  it can not be enjoyed , even on the level that one enjoys a bad slasher flick , primarily because it is dull .  a taut psychological thriller that does n't waste a moment of its two-hour running time .  a study in shades of gray , offering itself up in subtle plot maneuvers ...  the far future may be awesome to consider , but from period detail to matters of the heart , this film is most transporting when it stays put in the past .

joined_reference: if the first men in black was money , the second is small change .  charles ' entertaining film chronicles seinfeld 's return to stand-up comedy after the wrap of his legendary sitcom , alongside wannabe comic adams ' attempts to get his shot at the big time .  if the movie succeeds in instilling a wary sense of ` there but for the grace of god , ' it is far too self-conscious to draw you deeply into its world .  ... designed to provide a mix of smiles and tears , `` crossroads '' instead provokes a handful of unintentional howlers and numerous yawns .  whether you like rap music or loathe it , you ca n't deny either the tragic loss of two young men in the prime of their talent or the power of this movie .  with tightly organized efficiency , numerous flashbacks and a constant edge of tension , miller 's film is one of 2002 's involvingly adult surprises .  the minor figures surrounding ( bobby ) ... form a gritty urban mosaic .  vera 's three actors -- mollà , gil and bardem -- excel in insightful , empathetic performances .  it 's great escapist fun that recreates a place and time that will never happen again .  the son 's room is a triumph of gentility that earns its moments of pathos .  but the power of these ( subjects ) is obscured by the majority of the film that shows a stationary camera on a subject that could be mistaken for giving a public oration , rather than contributing to a film 's narrative .  it 's one pussy-ass world when even killer-thrillers revolve around group therapy sessions .  stealing harvard aspires to comedic grand larceny but stands convicted of nothing more than petty theft of your time .  light years / several warp speeds / levels and levels of dilithium crystals better than the pitiful insurrection .  the director knows how to apply textural gloss , but his portrait of sex-as-war is strictly sitcom .  so , too , is this comedy about mild culture clashing in today 's new delhi .  deliriously funny , fast and loose , accessible to the uninitiated , and full of surprises  filmmakers who can deftly change moods are treasures and even marvels .  although laced with humor and a few fanciful touches , the film is a refreshingly serious look at young women .  so devoid of any kind of intelligible story that it makes films like xxx and collateral damage seem like thoughtful treatises  the film is beautifully mounted , but , more to the point , the issues are subtly presented , managing to walk a fine line with regard to the question of joan 's madness .  makes for a pretty unpleasant viewing experience .  late marriage 's stiffness is unlikely to demonstrate the emotional clout to sweep u.s. viewers off their feet .  no sophomore slump for director sam mendes , who segues from oscar winner to oscar-winning potential with a smooth sleight of hand .  it 's too self-important and plodding to be funny , and too clipped and abbreviated to be an epic .  collateral damage finally delivers the goods for schwarzenegger fans .  ... mafia , rap stars and hood rats butt their ugly heads in a regurgitation of cinematic violence that gives brutal birth to an unlikely , but likable , hero . '  detox is ultimately a pointless endeavor .  nicks , seemingly uncertain what 's going to make people laugh , runs the gamut from stale parody to raunchy sex gags to formula romantic comedy .  the band 's courage in the face of official repression is inspiring , especially for aging hippies ( this one included ) .  the mesmerizing performances of the leads keep the film grounded and keep the audience riveted .  all that 's missing is the spontaneity , originality and delight .  portentous and pretentious , the weight of water is appropriately titled , given the heavy-handedness of it drama .  outer-space buffs might love this film , but others will find its pleasures intermittent .  offers very little genuine romance and even fewer laughs ... a sad sitcom of a movie , largely devoid of charm .  every nanosecond of the the new guy reminds you that you could be doing something else far more pleasurable .  suffocated by its fussy script and uptight characters , this musty adaptation is all the more annoying since it 's been packaged and sold back to us by hollywood .  its well of thorn and vinegar ( and simple humanity ) has long been plundered by similar works featuring the insight and punch this picture so conspicuously lacks .  i had to look away - this was god awful .  drops you into a dizzying , volatile , pressure-cooker of a situation that quickly snowballs out of control , while focusing on the what much more than the why .  it all adds up to good fun .  at its best , queen is campy fun like the vincent price horror classics of the '60s .  to say this was done better in wilder 's some like it hot is like saying the sun rises in the east .  when the film ended , i felt tired and drained and wanted to lie on my own deathbed for a while .  green might want to hang onto that ski mask , as robbery may be the only way to pay for his next project .  a film about a young man finding god that is accessible and touching to the marrow .  this flick is about as cool and crowd-pleasing as a documentary can get .  although huppert 's intensity and focus has a raw exhilaration about it , the piano teacher is anything but fun .  despite its title , punch-drunk love is never heavy-handed .  movie fans , get ready to take off ... the other direction .  i 'll bet the video game is a lot more fun than the film .  a beguiling splash of pastel colors and prankish comedy from disney .  a romantic comedy enriched by a sharp eye for manners and mores .  made with no discernible craft and monstrously sanctimonious in dealing with childhood loss .  the humor is forced and heavy-handed , and occasionally simply unpleasant .  not since tom cruise in risky business has an actor made such a strong impression in his underwear .  while the ideas about techno-saturation are far from novel , they 're presented with a wry dark humor .  thanks to haynes ' absolute control of the film 's mood , and buoyed by three terrific performances , far from heaven actually pulls off this stylistic juggling act .  may be far from the best of the series , but it 's assured , wonderfully respectful of its past and thrilling enough to make it abundantly clear that this movie phenomenon has once again reinvented itself for a new generation .  the film 's welcome breeziness and some unbelievably hilarious moments -- most portraying the idiocy of the film industry -- make it mostly worth the trip .  nelson 's brutally unsentimental approach ... sucks the humanity from the film , leaving behind an horrific but weirdly unemotional spectacle .  a coda in every sense , the pinochet case splits time between a minute-by-minute account of the british court 's extradition chess game and the regime 's talking-head survivors .  ` easily my choice for one of the year 's best films . '  a spellbinding african film about the modern condition of rootlessness , a state experienced by millions around the globe .

joined_reference: or doing last year 's taxes with your ex-wife .  impostor has a handful of thrilling moments and a couple of good performances , but the movie does n't quite fly .  a nightmare date with a half-formed wit done a great disservice by a lack of critical distance and a sad trust in liberal arts college bumper sticker platitudes .  the chateau cleverly probes the cross-cultural differences between gauls and yanks .  his last movie was poetically romantic and full of indelible images , but his latest has nothing going for it .  at its worst , it implodes in a series of very bad special effects .  chokes on its own depiction of upper-crust decorum .  displaying about equal amounts of naiveté , passion and talent , beneath clouds establishes sen as a filmmaker of considerable potential .  this time mr. burns is trying something in the martin scorsese street-realist mode , but his self-regarding sentimentality trips him up again .  the sort of film that makes me miss hitchcock , but also feel optimistic that there 's hope for popular cinema yet .  allows us to hope that nolan is poised to embark a major career as a commercial yet inventive filmmaker .  stealing harvard is evidence that the farrelly bros. -- peter and bobby -- and their brand of screen comedy are wheezing to an end , along with green 's half-hearted movie career .  a by-the-numbers patient/doctor pic that covers all the usual ground  if you 've ever entertained the notion of doing what the title of this film implies , what sex with strangers actually shows may put you off the idea forever .  it 's so good that its relentless , polished wit can withstand not only inept school productions , but even oliver parker 's movie adaptation .  by getting myself wrapped up in the visuals and eccentricities of many of the characters , i found myself confused when it came time to get to the heart of the movie .  harrison 's flowers puts its heart in the right place , but its brains are in no particular place at all .  this surreal gilliam-esque film is also a troubling interpretation of ecclesiastes .  nothing in waking up in reno ever inspired me to think of its inhabitants as anything more than markers in a screenplay .  the story and the friendship proceeds in such a way that you 're watching a soap opera rather than a chronicle of the ups and downs that accompany lifelong friendships .  the film will play equally well on both the standard and giant screens .  the movie 's accumulated force still feels like an ugly knot tightening in your stomach .  few films capture so perfectly the hopes and dreams of little boys on baseball fields as well as the grown men who sit in the stands .  comes off like a rejected abc afterschool special , freshened up by the dunce of a screenwriting 101 class .  it 's of the quality of a lesser harrison ford movie - six days , seven nights , maybe , or that dreadful sabrina remake .  a lackluster , unessential sequel to the classic disney adaptation of j.m. barrie 's peter pan .  the entire movie is about a boring , sad man being boring and sad .  the film 's hackneyed message is not helped by the thin characterizations , nonexistent plot and pretentious visual style .  this is a train wreck of an action film -- a stupefying attempt by the filmmakers to force-feed james bond into the mindless xxx mold and throw 40 years of cinematic history down the toilet in favor of bright flashes and loud bangs .  feels too formulaic and too familiar to produce the transgressive thrills of early underground work .  there are simply too many ideas floating around -- part farce , part sliding doors , part pop video -- and yet failing to exploit them .  yakusho and shimizu ... create engaging characterizations in imamura 's lively and enjoyable cultural mix .  a swashbuckling tale of love , betrayal , revenge and above all , faith .  we have n't seen such hilarity since say it is n't so !  attempts by this ensemble film to impart a message are so heavy-handed that they instead pummel the audience .  a very long movie , dull in stretches , with entirely too much focus on meal preparation and igloo construction .  like mike is a winner for kids , and no doubt a winner for lil bow wow , who can now add movies to the list of things he does well .  the tale of tok ( andy lau ) , a sleek sociopath on the trail of o ( takashi sorimachi ) , the most legendary of asian hitmen , is too scattershot to take hold .  a tv style murder mystery with a few big screen moments ( including one that seems to be made for a different film altogether ) .  may reawaken discussion of the kennedy assassination but this fictional film looks made for cable rather than for the big screen .  a superbly acted and funny/gritty fable of the humanizing of one woman at the hands of the unseen forces of fate .  a tender , heartfelt family drama .  while its careful pace and seemingly opaque story may not satisfy every moviegoer 's appetite , the film 's final scene is soaringly , transparently moving .  birthday girl is an amusing joy ride , with some surprisingly violent moments .  for anyone unfamiliar with pentacostal practices in general and theatrical phenomenon of hell houses in particular , it 's an eye-opener .  it 's a grab bag of genres that do n't add up to a whole lot of sense .  something akin to a japanese alice through the looking glass , except that it seems to take itself far more seriously .  that dogged good will of the parents and ` vain ' jia 's defoliation of ego , make the film touching despite some doldrums .  visually rather stunning , but ultimately a handsome-looking bore , the true creativity would have been to hide treasure planet entirely and completely reimagine it .  almost gags on its own gore .  the characters are interesting and often very creatively constructed from figure to backstory .  not only unfunny , but downright repellent .  this is wild surreal stuff , but brilliant and the camera just kind of sits there and lets you look at this and its like you 're going from one room to the next and none of them have any relation to the other .  this is a story of two misfits who do n't stand a chance alone , but together they are magnificent .  at the very least , if you do n't know anything about derrida when you walk into the theater , you wo n't know much more when you leave .  his comedy premises are often hackneyed or just plain crude , calculated to provoke shocked laughter , without following up on a deeper level .  the moviegoing equivalent of going to a dinner party and being forced to watch the host and hostess 's home video of their baby 's birth .  trite , banal , cliched , mostly inoffensive .  a warm but realistic meditation on friendship , family and affection .  majidi is an unconventional storyteller , capable of finding beauty in the most depressing places .  there is no pleasure in watching a child suffer .  expect the same-old , lame-old slasher nonsense , just with different scenery .  without non-stop techno or the existential overtones of a kieslowski morality tale , maelström is just another winter sleepers .  woody allen 's latest is an ambling , broad comedy about all there is to love -- and hate -- about the movie biz .

joined_reference: a breezy romantic comedy that has the punch of a good sitcom , while offering exceptionally well-detailed characters .  ... takes the beauty of baseball and melds it with a story that could touch anyone regardless of their familiarity with the sport  i can take infantile humor ... but this is the sort of infantile that makes you wonder about changing the director and writer 's diapers .  very psychoanalytical -- provocatively so -- and also refreshingly literary .  it 's a scattershot affair , but when it hits its mark it 's brilliant .  a strangely compelling and brilliantly acted psychological drama .  it 's a bad thing when a movie has about as much substance as its end credits blooper reel .  one of the most significant moviegoing pleasures of the year .  this one is definitely one to skip , even for horror movie fanatics .  in execution , this clever idea is far less funny than the original , killers from space .  the notion that bombing buildings is the funniest thing in the world goes entirely unexamined in this startlingly unfunny comedy .  sit through this one , and you wo n't need a magic watch to stop time ; your dvd player will do it for you .  pumpkin means to be an outrageous dark satire on fraternity life , but its ambitions far exceed the abilities of writer adam larson broder and his co-director , tony r. abrams , in their feature debut .  a woman 's pic directed with resonance by ilya chaiken .  it 's a cookie-cutter movie , a cut-and-paste job .  preaches to two completely different choirs at the same time , which is a pretty amazing accomplishment .  morton uses her face and her body language to bring us morvern 's soul , even though the character is almost completely deadpan .  even with a green mohawk and a sheet of fire-red flame tattoos covering his shoulder , however , kilmer seems to be posing , rather than acting .  funny but perilously slight .  ramsay , as in ratcatcher , remains a filmmaker with an acid viewpoint and a real gift for teasing chilly poetry out of lives and settings that might otherwise seem drab and sordid .  sacrifices the value of its wealth of archival foot-age with its less-than-objective stance .  determined to be fun , and bouncy , with energetic musicals , the humor did n't quite engage this adult .  a sometimes tedious film .  due to some script weaknesses and the casting of the director 's brother , the film trails off into inconsequentiality .  a very well-made , funny and entertaining picture .  one of creepiest , scariest movies to come along in a long , long time , easily rivaling blair witch or the others .  fancy a real downer ?  a warm , funny , engaging film .  the lion king was a roaring success when it was released eight years ago , but on imax it seems better , not just bigger .  the best that can be said about the work here of scottish director ritchie ... is that he obviously does n't have his heart in it .  affleck and jackson are good sparring partners .  the socio-histo-political treatise is told in earnest strides ... ( and ) personal illusion is deconstructed with poignancy .  unlike the speedy wham-bam effect of most hollywood offerings , character development -- and more importantly , character empathy -- is at the heart of italian for beginners .  bennett 's naturalistic performance speaks volumes more truth than any ` reality ' show , and anybody contemplating their own drastic life changes should watch some body first .  scooby dooby doo / and shaggy too / you both look and sound great .  ... is an arthritic attempt at directing by callie khouri .  worth watching for dong jie 's performance -- and for the way it documents a culture in the throes of rapid change .  his healthy sense of satire is light and fun ...  this illuminating documentary transcends our preconceived vision of the holy land and its inhabitants , revealing the human complexities beneath .  a quiet , pure , elliptical film  too often , the viewer is n't reacting to humor so much as they are wincing back in repugnance .  based on a devilishly witty script by heather mcgowan and niels mueller , the film gets great laughs , but never at the expense of its characters  ... a hollow joke told by a cinematic gymnast having too much fun embellishing the misanthropic tale to actually engage it .  what was once original has been co-opted so frequently that it now seems pedestrian .  because of an unnecessary and clumsy last scene , ` swimfan ' left me with a very bad feeling .  a giggle-inducing comedy with snappy dialogue and winning performances by an unlikely team of oscar-winners : susan sarandon and goldie hawn .  seldom has a movie so closely matched the spirit of a man and his work .  what really makes it special is that it pulls us into its world , gives us a hero whose suffering and triumphs we can share , surrounds him with interesting characters and sends us out of the theater feeling we 've shared a great adventure .  it 's another stale , kill-by-numbers flick , complete with blade-thin characters and terrible , pun-laden dialogue .  while it 's genuinely cool to hear characters talk about early rap records ( sugar hill gang , etc. ) , the constant referencing of hip-hop arcana can alienate even the savviest audiences .  for the most part , director anne-sophie birot 's first feature is a sensitive , extraordinarily well-acted drama .  a fitfully amusing romp that , if nothing else , will appeal to fans of malcolm in the middle and its pubescent star , frankie muniz .  a tender , witty , captivating film about friendship , love , memory , trust and loyalty .  one of the best films of the year with its exploration of the obstacles to happiness faced by five contemporary individuals ... a psychological masterpiece .  it ca n't decide if it wants to be a mystery/thriller , a romance or a comedy .  a giggle a minute .  this piece of channel 5 grade trash is , quite frankly , an insult to the intelligence of the true genre enthusiast .  mattei is tiresomely grave and long-winded , as if circularity itself indicated profundity .  it 's a beautiful madness .  ... the movie is just a plain old monster .  writer-director 's mehta 's effort has tons of charm and the whimsy is in the mixture , the intoxicating masala , of cultures and film genres .  an absurdist comedy about alienation , separation and loss .  though it 's become almost redundant to say so , major kudos go to leigh for actually casting people who look working-class .  schaeffer has to find some hook on which to hang his persistently useless movies , and it might as well be the resuscitation of the middle-aged character .
